action.reasoning: Observation: Iter9 train.py crashed with KeyError: tensor(53) in target_transform because dict lookup used tensor key instead of int. Analysis: The mapping dictionary keys are ints, but the label tensor hashed differently. Need to convert label to int via .item(). Also earlier CUDA error (iter7) was likely due to label out-of-range because target_transform wasn't applied correctly. Decision: Rewrite train.py with robust internal label mapping (.item()), global internal mapping for all seen classes, corrected distillation slicing, proper replay integration, NME evaluation, and metric logging. Run a quick smoke test (1 epoch/task) to validate pipeline, then if successful run full 30 epochs/task training and submit eval.
